name: PatientPoint Patient Engagement API
what_it_does: PatientPoint point-of-care patient engagement platform API for delivering targeted health content, patient education, and pharma-sponsored wellness programs in clinical settings. Enables AI agents to manage patient education content delivery and clinical condition targeting for point-of-care education automation, handle health content personalization based on diagnosis and demographics for relevant patient engagement, access provider network and clinical location management for distributed content deployment, retrieve patient engagement analytics and content interaction data for health communication measurement, manage pharma brand content campaigns and condition-specific education for manufacturer engagement programs, handle consent-aware health communication targeting for patient-appropriate messaging, access wellness program enrollment and tracking for population health initiatives, retrieve clinical protocol alignment data for evidence-based patient education, manage multi-language health content delivery for health literacy and equity programs, and integrate PatientPoint with EHR, practice management, and population health platforms.
use_cases: Delivering patient education from point-of-care content agents
Targeting health content from condition-specific engagement agents
Measuring engagement from patient communication analytics agents
Managing pharma campaigns from healthcare media agents
not_for: Direct-to-consumer patient communication (use patient portal platforms)
Clinical decision support requiring FDA SaMD clearance
Inpatient hospital patient education without ambulatory focus
best_when: A health system, medical practice, or pharmaceutical brand partner wanting AI agents to automate point-of-care patient education, condition-specific content targeting, health literacy programs, and engagement analytics in clinical settings.
avoid_when: HIPAA MINIMUM NECESSARY AND PHI USE LIMITATION: PatientPoint processes patient encounter context including diagnosis and demographics for content targeting; Business Associate Agreement required; automated content targeting using PHI must comply with HIPAA minimum necessary and treatment/operations purpose limitation. FTC health advertising compliance — pharma-sponsored health content delivered at point-of-care must comply with FTC advertising disclosure requirements; automated content delivery without clear sponsorship disclosure creates FTC deceptive advertising risk. FDA promotional material compliance — automated delivery of pharma brand content must comply with FDA promotional material requirements including fair balance and required risk information; automated pharma content without FDA-compliant safety information creates regulatory violation. Condition targeting and health equity — automated content targeting by clinical condition must not create disparate access to health information; automated algorithms that systematically under-serve certain patient populations create health equity risk.

auth.notes: PatientPoint uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes. REST API with JSON. Cincinnati, Ohio HQ. Founded 2008. Private (Warburg Pincus PE). Point-of-care patient engagement and health media network. 140,000+ provider touch points across 30,000+ clinical locations. Pharmaceutical brand partner programs at point of care. Competes with Outcome Health and in-office patient education platforms.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — provider network subscription and pharma partnership pricing
pricing.paid_tiers: Provider network subscription; pharma brand partnership campaigns priced separately
pricing.notes: Cincinnati OH. Warburg Pincus PE. Founded 2008. 140,000+ provider touch points. 30,000+ clinical locations. Pharmaceutical brand partnership model.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; content delivery latency requirements for point-of-care context

known_agent_gotchas: HIPAA PHI USE LIMITATION FOR CONTENT TARGETING: Automated patient education targeting using diagnosis or demographic PHI must be limited to treatment and healthcare operations purposes; automated pharma brand targeting using PHI without patient authorization creates HIPAA marketing restriction violation

FDA promotional material fair balance requirement — automated pharma-sponsored content delivery must include required safety information and fair balance for prescription drug promotion; automated delivery of branded content without required risk information creates FDA promotional violation
No webhooks — patient engagement event completion and content interaction events require polling; no push notification for individual patient content interaction triggers

Pharma brand content approval chain — automated pharma sponsor content must be MLR (medical, legal, regulatory) approved before deployment; automated content publishing without MLR approval creates FDA promotional material violation

Health equity content gap monitoring — automated condition-specific content delivery must be monitored for systematic gaps in coverage for specific patient populations; automated algorithms without equity monitoring create health disparity amplification risk

Clinical location network latency — PatientPoint content delivery to clinical settings relies on in-office display network; automated content updates require propagation time to distributed endpoint devices; real-time automated targeting must account for display network propagation delay
agent_readiness.security_notes: Point-of-care engagement. HIPAA, SOC2. OAuth2 with scopes. US. PHI and patient education targeting data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Patient engagement. HIPAA, SOC2. OAuth2 with scopes. US. PHI and clinical engagement data.
